column1: # Old Mojang name → New Mojang name (26.1 package reorganization)
# ResourceLocation$Serializer removed in 26.1 — redirect to Retromod polyfill
# CycleOption → OptionInstance (removed in 1.19, but intermediary refs still exist)
# Button$OnTooltip removed (tooltips now use components directly)
# GuiComponent removed — redirect to GuiGraphicsExtractor (closest equivalent)
# LazyLoadedValue was deprecated (use Suppliers.memoize), NOT renamed to Unit
# Keep it as-is — if it doesn't exist in 26.1, a polyfill should handle it
# 26.1 Weighted* class consolidation. Mojang unified WeightedRandomList
# Inner classes repackaged under WeightedList$Builder etc. Without these
# moves old mods (Lithium, Architectury, etc.) hit NoClassDefFoundError at
# runtime when they try to reference the old class names.
# Composed by diffing this environment's MC 1.20.1 official class names
# (from Mojang client/server mappings) against the 26.1.2 client jar's
# real (unobfuscated) class names, then matching moved classes by simple
# name. Each entry was confirmed real by EITHER belonging to a systematic
# package-relocation cluster (>=3 classes moving the same package together)
# OR >=50% member-name overlap (1.20.1 mappings vs 26.1.2 bytecode) OR a
# move into the class's own sub-package. 6 ambiguous cross-domain singletons
# were deliberately left out pending review. These fill gaps the original
# hand-curated list missed (e.g. options screens -> screens/options, game
# Same method as the 1.20.1 block above, but baselined on MC 1.16.5 — the
# oldest non-experimental version Retromod targets, so 1.16.5-era mods get
# the same move coverage as newer ones. Diffed 1.16.5 official class names
# vs the 26.1.2 client jar, matched by simple name, kept only moves confirmed
# by a >=3-class package cluster, >=50% member overlap, or a same-subtree
# move. 14 coincidental simple-name collisions were rejected (e.g. tags/Tag
# vs nbt/Tag, block material/Material vs sprite Material). Largest clusters:
# worldgen structure classes -> structure/structures and -> structure/pools.
# ...). The package-move harvests above only matched same-simple-name classes;
# these were paired by member-set similarity instead: a disappeared old class
# is matched to the 26.1.2 class sharing the most member names, accepted only
# at >=70% coverage of the smaller member set, >=0.5 Jaccard, and a clearly
# unique best match. Anonymous inner classes ($1, $2) were excluded. Applied
# host-version-aware at runtime, so each lands only where the host already has
# the NEW name (a 1.21.11 host gets the subset that renamed by 1.21.11).
# Manual renames the member-matcher missed (heavily refactored classes whose
# member sets diverged, but the rename is verified against 1.21.11/26.1.2):
# --- snapshot.2: additional 26.1 package moves found by the compat audit ---
# GlStateManager moved com.mojang.blaze3d.platform → .opengl in the 26.1 render
# rewrite. The outer class moved cleanly (top compat-audit gap, ~31 refs).
# Its inner blend-factor enums were PROMOTED to top-level in the same platform
# DestFactor. The intermediary harvest (class_4535/class_4534) still emits the
# old inner names, so map them here. Enum constants (SRC_ALPHA, …) are unchanged,
# so this is a clean type move. Sole-blocker for 4 mods in the 5000-mod audit.
# Yarn name Item$Settings → Mojang Item$Properties. A handful of otherwise
# fully-remapped Fabric content mods (Macaw's furniture family, farmer's knives,
# runes) carry a stray Yarn *class* reference to Item.Settings while their method
# calls are intermediary (remapped fine) — so the class rename is the sole gap.
# Top sole-blocker (6+ mods) in the 5000-mod audit.
# VillagerTrades moved world.entity.npc → world.item.trading in 26.1. Outer type
# resolves; the $ItemListing inner interface was removed (custom-trade mods that
# implement it need re-authoring — tracked as a known limitation).
# BlockRenderLayerMap removed from the 26.1-era Fabric API → Retromod no-op stand-in
# (FabricBlockRenderLayerBridge injects the synthetic). Non-vanilla target: applied
# only on the 26.1+ path (where the real class is gone). Soft-fail — render-layer
# registration is inert (transparent blocks render solid), but the mod loads.
